title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miranda tells Mr. Polo and Michael Gaffield that she is searching for a legendary wishing well in the forest near the old quarry. She mentions that Grandma Goodbody, whom she calls a witch, claims the well was sealed up years ago because it was dangerous, possibly after a child named Kitty fell in. Michael Gaffield tells Miranda that while there is a regular well in White Hill, the legend of the wishing well is likely a story made up by Mrs. Goodbody. He reveals to Poirot that there was actually a wishing tree—a beech tree on a hill—that people circled to make wishes, but it was split by lightning six years ago.

After Miranda leaves, Poirot and Gaffield discuss Gaffield’s restlessness. Gaffield, a designer of houses and gardens, expresses dissatisfaction with his work and a desire to move, possibly to Greece, because clients like Drayton insist on impractical or showy designs rather than following his professional suggestions. 

The conversation shifts to the sudden death of Leslie Firle, a junior lawyer from a Manchester firm. Gaffield explains that Leslie was killed with a knife, and local speculation suggests it was due to a jealous affair with a woman named Sandra. Poirot inquires about Olga, suggesting a connection between her and Leslie. Gaffield confirms that Leslie and Olga were seen together often, though Olga is cautious and has not confided in him. Gaffield notes that Leslie was a charming man who was involved with several women, which caused upset among his girlfriends. Poirot expresses interest in investigating the events and "unsavory things" that occurred before Leslie and Olga met.
